items.title: Waarom SC Heerenveen verder gaat zonder een hoofd scout
items.summary: SC Heerenveen heeft afscheid genomen van Kenneth Zandvliet en gaat geen opvolger aanstellen voor de hoofd scout. Technisch manager Johan Hansma vertelt over de structuurwijziging in het Friese scoutingsteam en welk idee daarachter zit.

items.title: VI Live: AZ gaat met vier tegengoals van Benfica rusten in Youth League
items.summary: Woensdagavond vinden de laatste returns in de tussenronde van de Champions League plaats. Real Madrid en Paris Saint-Germain hopen het klusje te klaren. Atalanta en Juventus hebben een comeback nodig. Volg de duels in deze VI Live.

items.title: Faber reageert op 'terugkeer': 'Storm in een glas water'
items.summary: Ernest Faber stond woensdag weer op het trainingsveld bij Heracles Almelo. Maandag werd bekend dat de trainer het stokje voor twee dagen overdroeg aan zijn technische staf en dat hij zich op het technisch directeurschap zou richten bij de club in crisis. Dat verhaal ligt volgens Faber een stuk genuanceerder.

items.title: Lionel Messi 'deeply regrets' not learning English
items.summary: Inter Miami superstar Lionel Messi has spoken of his regret over not applying himself in his studies when he was young, particularly when learning English.

items.title: Sources: Coutinho mulling MLS move or retirement
items.summary: Philippe Coutinho is considering the prospect of a move to MLS or retiring from playing at the age of 33, a source have told ESPN.

items.title: Mbappé (knee) out of Madrid's game with Benfica
items.summary: Kylian Mbappé has not been named in Real Madrid's squad for their Champions League playoff second leg with Benfica

items.title: Scotland fans given all-clear to wear their sporrans at World Cup matches
items.summary: Fifa to allow traditional Highland accessory into grounds Tournament rules only permit certain types of bags Scotland fans have been given the all-clear to wear their sporrans at the team’s matches at the 2026 World Cup. Tournament rules only permitted certain types of bags into stadiums, and the pouch traditionally worn by Scots at the front of their kilt was deemed too large to meet the strict criteria. Continue reading...

items.title: UEFA geeft Benfica nul op rekest: Prestianni definitief niet tegen Real Madrid
items.summary: Gianluca Prestianni is woensdagavond definitief niet van de partij in de return van de tussenronde van de Champions League tegen Real Madrid. Benfica had beroep aangetekend tegen de veelbesproken schorsing van de Argentijn, maar heeft geen gehoor gekregen van de UEFA.

items.title: Uefa rejects Benfica appeal over Prestianni ban
items.summary: Gianluca Prestianni will miss the second leg of Benfica's Champions League tie against Real Madrid after Uefa reject the club's appeal against his suspension.

items.title: Bord consortium pull out of Sheff Wed takeover
items.summary: A consortium led by former professional poker player James Bord pulls out of a takeover of Sheffield Wednesday

items.title: Footballers are calling out racism because they have had enough. Those with power must act | Samuel Okafor
items.summary: The leadership shown by four Premier League players in highlighting racism last weekend must be replicated, and addressing representation is part of that Recent incidents involving Sir Jim Ratcliffe and Vinícius Júnior have offered a new layer to the question Kick It Out is often asked when discrimination occurs: is it getting worse or are more people reporting it? The question has been posed again this week after four Premier League players highlighted racist abuse sent to them on social media after matches last weekend. Continue reading...

items.title: F-Side blijft leeg bij duel tussen Sparta en Ajax
items.summary: Ajax moet het tegen Sparta Rotterdam stellen zonder een deel van het thuispubliek. De Amsterdammers hebben een schikkingsvoorstel van de onafhankelijke aanklager betaald voetbal geaccepteerd. De straf is naar aanleiding van afgestoken vuurwerk tegen FC Groningen.

items.title: F1 contracts: How long is each driver signed up for?
items.summary: A look at how long each Formula 1 driver's contract is ahead of the 2026 F1 season where several changes could take place for 2027.

items.title: Man Utd post profit but debt rises to £1.3bn
items.summary: The amount Manchester United owe increases to almost £1.3bn in outstanding transfer fees, rolling credit facility and legacy debt.

items.title: Champions League's biggest upsets: Bodo/Glimt join list of giant-killers
items.summary: Norwegian minnows Bodo/Glimt pulled off one of the biggest-ever UCL shocks. Here are 10 more.

items.title: Don't talk about winning: The secret behind Bodo/Glimt's European success
items.summary: Bodo/Glimt's Champions League win vs. Inter Milan is a major upset. The signs were there in last year's Europa League run.

items.title: Sheffield Wednesday takeover collapses as would-be buyers end their interest
items.summary: Consortium included Dunfermline co-owner James Bord They say due diligence showed their offer was far too high A consortium seeking to buy Sheffield Wednesday says it has withdrawn from the process. The consortium, which features James Bord, the co-owner of Dunfermline, was granted exclusivity by club administrators in December. However, it has released a statement confirming that interest is at an end. A spokesperson for the consortium said: “Throughout the process to purchase Sheffield Wednesday Football Club we have at all times respected the integrity of the process, working professionally, constructively and transparently with the administrator, the EFL and the IFR [Independent Football Regulator] to provide them with the details they need to satisfy their tests. Continue reading...

items.title: Consortium seeking to buy Sheff Wed withdraws interest
items.summary: A consortium seeking to buy Sheffield Wednesday says it has withdrawn from the process.

items.title: Constitution Hill ruled out of Champion Hurdle as hurdling career ends
items.summary: Constitution Hill has been ruled out of running in the Champion Hurdle at the Cheltenham Festival by trainer Nicky Henderson.

items.title: Bodø/Glimt bask in ‘crazy’ Champions League victory over shellshocked Inter
items.summary: Italian giants were well beaten by side who dazzled despite Norway’s domestic season not starting until next month There was a moment after the final whistle at San Siro on Tuesday night when the head coaches, Bodø/Glimt’s Kjetil Knutsen and Inter’s Cristian Chivu, stood chatting, seemingly discussing some tactical element of the game that had just finished. Chivu appeared genuinely interested in what Knutsen had to say, smiling politely, but above all he looked utterly bemused. What the hell had just happened? His Inter team, top of Serie A by 10 points and undefeated in the league since 23 November, had not only lost the home leg of their Champions League playoff against the Norwegian side but been well beaten: 2-1 on the night and 5-2 on aggregate. Continue reading...
